name: Project Model Compiler
summary: Validates durable project truth and its Development Plan, then compiles them into bounded worker context and verification obligations.

invariants: Dependencies are visible only through owned Contracts.
A supplied Context Capsule may narrow but never broaden generated scope.

Project Model alone compiles one opaque bounded Module path ownership product bound to the exact Model and exact tracked-path facts: Module and disposition selectors use only canonical exact paths or literal recursive prefixes with include-minus-exclude matching; every disposition has a stable id, kind ungoverned, canonical include and exclude lists, and substantive rationale; every latent or concrete owner-owner, owner-disposition, or disposition-disposition overlap fails without precedence; every tracked path has exactly one Module write owner or one disposition; and a disposition never grants write authority.

Generated Context Capsule write scope is projected only from the exact ownership product for its primary Module; absent, forged, stale, ambiguous, disposition-only, or over-limit inputs fail closed, and callers may narrow but never widen that scope.
The ownership product enters Change compilation only through a process-local options seam and is never serialized into worker, HTTP, Store, or browser input.
Unrelated Claims require an explicit authority-bound mapping.

Acceptance Evidence eligibility is compiled from canonical Claim Gate routes at exact Gate and command identity, filtered to routes applicable to the primary Module through acceptance-eligible Gates; cross-Claim mappings expose exact source routes, and integration-only full Gate routes never enter acceptance mappings.

Claim Gate routes compile in one bounded pass for the complete canonical requested Claim set; compileClaimGateRoutes delegates to that compiler, while Profile validation, Knowledge Gap validation, and graph projection consume the same authoritative product without reinterpreting Gate applicability.

A compiled Claim Gate route product is an opaque process-local handle bound by Project Model to the exact preflighted Model identity, a compiler-derived content digest, and canonical Claim coverage. projectCompiledClaimGateRouteIndex rejects forged, wrong-Model, content-drifted, or incomplete products, ignores caller-visible handle mutation, and returns isolated routes plus an immutable compiler-owned observation containing at least workUnits, routes, and totalRouteBytes; workUnits includes requested Claim normalization.

A route-product consumer may only tighten hard usage limits; projection fails closed against compiler-recorded usage before returning routes, and resource observations are neither persisted nor interpreted as assurance.

Project Model alone compiles one bounded batch module Claim/Gate projection from an opaque exact-Model-bound route product: requested Modules receive only Claims from their publicContracts and dependency via Contracts, explicit frozen-Plan route selections may add target or cross-source Claim pairs without widening authoring visibility, and both receive only acceptance-eligible original routes. Integration- or release-only full-Gate expansion is excluded, route shape, order, and canonical digest remain unchanged, all work shares one aggregate budget, and forged, wrong-Model, incomplete, drifted, serialized, malformed, unknown-Module, or unbounded inputs fail closed.

projectCompiledModuleClaimGateIndex returns claimsByModule, routesByModule, and an operational observation: moduleRefs alone drive authoring visibility, routeSelections may only add explicit Module and Claim pairs to routesByModule, every requested pair with no eligible route remains distinguishable as an empty route list, and canonical deduplication, ordering, and one aggregate budget apply across both input channels.

Architecture Profile accepts a supplied Claim Gate route product only through a separate in-memory options seam; serializable Profile input, Change facts, persisted records, HTTP or CLI payloads, and worker-authored data cannot supply or forge that product.

Change planRefs are a bounded strict value object: only absence, a non-empty string of at most 256 trimmed characters, or a dense list of at most 64 such strings is accepted, and every compiler projection uses the same trimmed, first-occurrence canonical Outcome references without discarding invalid entries.
An ordinary Change cannot compile against a missing, unknown, or non-active Development Outcome.
Stable Outcome Criteria exactly mirror exit criteria and their declared Claim and Knowledge Gap sets.
Outcome Contributions are derived only from exact Change Claims visible through the primary Module's owned or dependency Contracts; ambiguous matches require an explicit Criterion hint.
Enforced Outcome alignment admits only exact Contributions or explicit non-progress exception requests bound to frozen Development Plan authority.
Outcome status deltas compile only from an append-only ledger whose prior Accepted Package proofs, exact Claim Evidence, closed Gaps, and authority assessments cover the frozen route semantics.

Project Model alone compiles a bounded Outcome Plan Amendment from one frozen Governance Baseline and one candidate Model: planned or conditional definition changes require exactly one append-only Revision carrying the complete prior definition and digest, a compiler-derived current digest and changed-field set, substantive rationale, and frozen Plan authority; revision history is an exact prefix, an Amendment that appends any Revision cannot append any Outcome status Transition, activation freezes the latest frozen-baseline definition digest, and active or terminal definitions never change. Kernel compile, Gate, and acceptance rederive through the same Interface and callers never walk ledgers or compute their bindings.

A Knowledge Gap Closure Contract requires at least one executable Gate route per proof Claim and freezes exact proof Claim ownership, Gate and command identity, command, timeout, effective Module applicability, oracle, applicability, discriminatory power, and residual uncertainty while permitting unrelated Claims and raw scope edits that do not change that effective route; closure requires prior sealed implementation Contributions whose direct positive Gate Evidence covers every proof Claim.

A later-discovered prerequisite cannot rewrite an active Outcome's frozen Criteria or an existing Closure Contract: blocker relations are separate monotonic closure-consumption constraints, and canonical blocksGapClosureRefs plus exact blocksOutcomeTransitionRoutes may be declared or extended only by an open source Gap that already owns a governed Closure Contract, are append-only set inclusions against the frozen baseline, become fully frozen when the source closes, and reject unknown or already-closed Gap targets, duplicates, self-edges, cyclic Gap graphs, invalid Outcome routes, or declaration beside a matching target closure or Transition in the same amendment.

Outcome Transition Gap dispositions exactly cover frozen Criterion Gaps plus every matching baseline-open blocker and every required Gap's transitive baseline-open incoming blockers; a blocker clears only when its own open-to-closed delta, unchanged frozen Closure Contract, prior sealed implementation Contribution to the target Outcome, and direct positive proof Claim Evidence compile atomically in that Transition, never from a status edit alone, while a blocker already closed in the frozen baseline requires no disposition.
A newly declared Closure proof Claim uses a dedicated fixed test entrypoint that fails closed before its implementation can produce direct Evidence.

Architecture Acceptance Profiles recompute the authoritative Project Model content digest and are deterministic bounded typed graphs compiled only from that bound Model, exact Claim Gate routes, and Kernel-normalized snapshot facts; current and sealed-historical Evidence remain separate, Evidence-to-Claim edges are neutral obligation-bound associations rather than support or approval conclusions, one obligation may retain multiple distinct exact source Claim routes only when its target, kind, obligation digest, and authority binding remain identical, Outcome Contributions are relevance-only, and no score, percentage, confidence, green-light, coverage, or Criterion-satisfaction conclusion is emitted.
Change Kernel create and patch adapters must reject malformed or conflicting planRefs inputs before JSON persistence and store only the canonical compiler value.
